name: RhinoViewport
summary: Displays geometry with a given projection. In standard modeling views there is a one to one relationship between RhinoView and RhinoViewports. In a page layout, there may be multiple RhinoViewports for a single layout.

constructors.name: RhinoViewport()
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the RhinoViewport class

constructors.name: RhinoViewport(RhinoViewport)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the RhinoViewport class

methods.name: ChangeToParallelProjection
methods.summary: Use this function to change projections of valid viewports from perspective to parallel. It will make common additional adjustments to the frustum so the resulting views are similar. The camera location and direction will not be changed.

params.name: symmetricFrustum
params.doc: true if you want the resulting frustum to be symmetric.

returns.doc: If the current projection is parallel and bSymmetricFrustum, FrustumIsLeftRightSymmetric() and FrustumIsTopBottomSymmetric() are all equal, then no changes are made and true is returned.

methods.name: ChangeToPerspectiveProjection(Boolean, Double)
methods.signature: public bool ChangeToPerspectiveProjection(bool symmetricFrustum, double lensLength)
methods.summary: Use this function to change projections of valid viewports from parallel to perspective. It will make common additional adjustments to the frustum and camera location so the resulting views are similar. The camera direction and target point are not be changed.

params.name: symmetricFrustum
params.doc: true if you want the resulting frustum to be symmetric.

params.name: lensLength
params.doc: (pass 50.0 when in doubt) 35 mm lens length to use when changing from parallel to perspective projections. If the current projection is perspective or lens_length is <= 0.0, then this parameter is ignored.

returns.doc: If the current projection is perspective and bSymmetricFrustum, FrustumIsLeftRightSymmetric() and FrustumIsTopBottomSymmetric() are all equal, then no changes are made and true is returned.

methods.name: ChangeToPerspectiveProjection(Double, Boolean, Double)
methods.signature: public bool ChangeToPerspectiveProjection(double targetDistance, bool symmetricFrustum, double lensLength)
methods.summary: Use this function to change projections of valid viewports from parallel to perspective. It will make common additional adjustments to the frustum and camera location so the resulting views are similar. The camera direction and target point are not be changed.

params.name: targetDistance
params.doc: If RhinoMath.UnsetValue this parameter is ignored. Otherwise it must be > 0 and indicates which plane in the current view frustum should be preserved.

params.name: symmetricFrustum
params.doc: true if you want the resulting frustum to be symmetric.

params.name: lensLength
params.doc: (pass 50.0 when in doubt) 35 mm lens length to use when changing from parallel to perspective projections. If the current projection is perspective or lens_length is <= 0.0, then this parameter is ignored.

returns.doc: If the current projection is perspective and bSymmetricFrustum, FrustumIsLeftRightSymmetric() and FrustumIsTopBottomSymmetric() are all equal, then no changes are made and true is returned.

methods.name: ChangeToTwoPointPerspectiveProjection(Double)
methods.summary: Use this function to change projections of valid viewports to a two point perspective. It will make common additional adjustments to the frustum and camera location and direction so the resulting views are similar.

params.name: lensLength
params.doc: (pass 50.0 when in doubt) 35 mm lens length to use when changing from parallel to perspective projections. If the current projection is perspective or lens_length is <= 0.0, then this parameter is ignored.

returns.doc: If the current projection is perspective and bSymmetricFrustum, FrustumIsLeftRightSymmetric() and FrustumIsTopBottomSymmetric() are all equal, then no changes are made and true is returned.

methods.name: ChangeToTwoPointPerspectiveProjection(Double, Vector3d, Double)
methods.signature: public bool ChangeToTwoPointPerspectiveProjection(double targetDistance, Vector3d up, double lensLength)
methods.summary: Use this function to change projections of valid viewports to a two point perspective. It will make common additional adjustments to the frustum and camera location and direction so the resulting views are similar.

params.name: targetDistance
params.doc: If RhinoMath.UnsetValue this parameter is ignored. Otherwise it must be > 0 and indicates which plane in the current view frustum should be preserved.

params.name: up
params.doc: This direction will be the locked up direction. Pass ON_3dVector::ZeroVector if you want to use the world axis direction that is closest to the current up direction. Pass CameraY() if you want to preserve the current up direction.

params.name: lensLength
params.doc: (pass 50.0 when in doubt) 35 mm lens length to use when changing from parallel to perspective projections. If the current projection is perspective or lens_length is <= 0.0, then this parameter is ignored.

returns.doc: If the current projection is perspective and bSymmetricFrustum, FrustumIsLeftRightSymmetric() and FrustumIsTopBottomSymmetric() are all equal, then no changes are made and true is returned.

methods.summary: Remove trace image (background bitmap) for this viewport if one exists.

methods.summary: Releases all resources used by the RhinoViewport

methods.summary: Releases the unmanaged resources used by the RhinoViewport and optionally releases the managed resources

params.name: disposing
params.doc: True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

returns.doc: Returns a RhinoViewport if the Id is found otherwise null.

methods.summary: Gets the camera plane.

params.name: frame

returns.doc: true if current camera orientation is valid.

methods.name: GetDepth(Point3d, Double)
methods.signature: public bool GetDepth(Point3d point, out double distance)
methods.summary: Gets clipping distance of a point.

params.name: distance
params.doc: A computed distance is assigned to this out parameter if this call succeeds.

returns.doc: true if the point is in the view frustum and near_dist/far_dist were set. false if the bounding box does not intersect the view frustum.

methods.name: GetDepth(BoundingBox, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool GetDepth(BoundingBox bbox, out double nearDistance, out double farDistance)
methods.summary: Gets near and far clipping distances of a bounding box.

params.name: nearDistance
params.doc: The near distance is assigned to this out parameter during this call.

params.name: farDistance
params.doc: The far distance is assigned to this out parameter during this call.

returns.doc: true if the bounding box intersects the view frustum and near_dist/far_dist were set. false if the bounding box does not intersect the view frustum.

methods.name: GetDepth(Sphere, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool GetDepth(Sphere sphere, out double nearDistance, out double farDistance)
methods.summary: Gets near and far clipping distances of a sphere.

params.name: nearDistance
params.doc: The near distance is assigned to this out parameter during this call.

params.name: farDistance
params.doc: The far distance is assigned to this out parameter during this call.

returns.doc: true if the sphere intersects the view frustum and near_dist/far_dist were set. false if the sphere does not intersect the view frustum.

methods.name: GetFarRect
methods.summary: Get corners of far clipping plane rectangle.

returns.doc: [left_bottom, right_bottom, left_top, right_top] points on success null on failure.

methods.name: GetFrustum
methods.signature: public bool GetFrustum(out double left, out double right, out double bottom, out double top, out double nearDistance, out double farDistance)
methods.summary: Gets the view frustum.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded.

methods.name: GetFrustumBottomPlane
methods.summary: Get bottom world frustum clipping plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: frustum bottom side clipping plane. The normal points into the visible region of the frustum. If the projection is perspective, the origin is at the camera location, otherwise the origin is the point on the plane that is closest to the camera location.

returns.doc: true if camera and frustum are valid and plane was set.
[Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Display.RhinoViewport.GetFrustumBoundingBox"]

methods.name: GetFrustumCenter
methods.summary: Returns world coordinates of frustum's center.

params.name: center
params.doc: The center coordinate is assigned to this out parameter if this call succeeds.

returns.doc: true if the center was successfully computed.

methods.name: GetFrustumFarPlane
methods.summary: Get far clipping plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: far clipping plane if camera and frustum are valid. The plane's frame is the same as the camera's frame. The origin is located at the intersection of the camera direction ray and the far clipping plane.

returns.doc: true if camera and frustum are valid.

methods.name: GetFrustumLeftPlane
methods.summary: Get left world frustum clipping plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: frustum left side clipping plane. The normal points into the visible region of the frustum. If the projection is perspective, the origin is at the camera location, otherwise the origin is the point on the plane that is closest to the camera location.

returns.doc: true if camera and frustum are valid and plane was set.

methods.name: GetFrustumLine
methods.signature: public bool GetFrustumLine(double screenX, double screenY, out Line worldLine)
methods.summary: Gets the world coordinate line in the view frustum that projects to a point on the screen.

params.name: worldLine
params.doc: 3d world coordinate line segment starting on the near clipping plane and ending on the far clipping plane.

returns.doc: true if successful. false if view projection or frustum is invalid.

methods.name: GetFrustumNearPlane
methods.summary: Get near clipping plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: near clipping plane if camera and frustum are valid. The plane's frame is the same as the camera's frame. The origin is located at the intersection of the camera direction ray and the near clipping plane.

returns.doc: true if camera and frustum are valid.

methods.name: GetFrustumRightPlane
methods.summary: Get right world frustum clipping plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: frustum right side clipping plane. The normal points into the visible region of the frustum. If the projection is perspective, the origin is at the camera location, otherwise the origin is the point on the plane that is closest to the camera location.

returns.doc: true if camera and frustum are valid and plane was set.

methods.name: GetFrustumTopPlane
methods.summary: Get top world frustum clipping plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: frustum top side clipping plane. The normal points into the visible region of the frustum. If the projection is perspective, the origin is at the camera location, otherwise the origin is the point on the plane that is closest to the camera location.

returns.doc: true if camera and frustum are valid and plane was set.

methods.name: GetNearRect
methods.summary: Get corners of near clipping plane rectangle.

returns.doc: [left_bottom, right_bottom, left_top, right_top] points on success null on failure.

methods.name: GetPickTransform(Point)
methods.summary: Takes a rectangle in screen coordinates and returns a transformation that maps the 3d frustum defined by the rectangle to a -1/+1 clipping coordinate box. This takes a single point and inflates it by Rhino.ApplicationSettings.ModelAidSettings.MousePickBoxRadius to define the screen rectangle.

returns.doc: A transformation matrix.

methods.name: GetPickTransform(Rectangle)
methods.summary: Takes a rectangle in screen coordinates and returns a transformation that maps the 3d frustum defined by the rectangle to a -1/+1 clipping coordinate box.

returns.doc: A transformation matrix.

methods.name: GetPickTransform(Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public Transform GetPickTransform(int clientX, int clientY)
methods.summary: Takes a rectangle in screen coordinates and returns a transformation that maps the 3d frustum defined by the rectangle to a -1/+1 clipping coordinate box. This takes a single point and inflates it by Rhino.ApplicationSettings.ModelAidSettings.MousePickBoxRadius to define the screen rectangle.

returns.doc: A transformation matrix.

methods.name: GetScreenPort
methods.signature: public bool GetScreenPort(out int portLeft, out int portRight, out int portBottom, out int portTop, out int portNear, out int portFar)
methods.summary: Location of viewport in pixels. These are provided so you can set the port you are using and get the appropriate transformations to and from screen space.

returns.doc: true if the operation is successful.

methods.name: GetTransform
methods.signature: public Transform GetTransform(CoordinateSystem sourceSystem, CoordinateSystem destinationSystem)
methods.summary: Gets a transform from origin coordinate system to a target coordinate system.

returns.doc: 4x4 transformation matrix (acts on the left) Identity matrix is returned if this function fails.

params.name: key

returns.doc: string associated with the key if successful. null if no key was found.

methods.name: GetUserStrings
methods.summary: Gets an independent copy of the collection of (user text key, user text value) pairs attached to this object.

returns.doc: A collection of key strings and values strings. This

methods.name: GetWorldToScreenScale
methods.signature: public bool GetWorldToScreenScale(Point3d pointInFrustum, out double pixelsPerUnit)
methods.summary: Gets the world to screen size scaling factor at a point in frustum.

params.name: pixelsPerUnit
params.doc: scale = number of pixels per world unit at the 3d point. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

returns.doc: true if the operation is successful.

methods.name: IsVisible(BoundingBox)
methods.summary: Returns true if some portion of a world coordinate bounding box is potentially visible in the viewing frustum.

params.name: bbox
params.doc: A bounding box that is tested for visibility.

returns.doc: true if the box is potentially visible; otherwise false.

methods.name: IsVisible(Point3d)
methods.summary: Determines if a world coordinate point is visible in the viewing frustum.

params.name: point
params.doc: A point that is tested for visibility.

returns.doc: true if the point is visible; otherwise false.

methods.name: KeyboardDolly
methods.signature: public bool KeyboardDolly(bool leftRight, double amount)
methods.summary: Emulates the keyboard arrow key in terms of interaction.

params.name: leftRight
params.doc: left/right dolly if true, up/down dolly if false.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: KeyboardDollyInOut
methods.summary: Emulates the keyboard arrow key in terms of interaction.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: KeyboardRotate
methods.signature: public bool KeyboardRotate(bool leftRight, double angleRadians)
methods.summary: Emulates the keyboard arrow key in terms of interaction.

params.name: leftRight
params.doc: left/right rotate if true, up/down rotate if false.

params.name: angleRadians
params.doc: If less than 0, rotation is to left or down. If greater than 0, rotation is to right or up.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: Magnify(Double, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Magnify(double magnificationFactor, bool mode)
methods.summary: Zooms or dollies in order to scale the viewport projection of observed objects.

params.name: mode
params.doc: false = perform a "dolly" magnification by moving the camera towards/away from the target so that the amount of the screen subtended by an object changes. true = perform a "zoom" magnification by adjusting the "lens" angle

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: Magnify(Double, Boolean, Point)
methods.signature: public bool Magnify(double magnificationFactor, bool mode, Point fixedScreenPoint)
methods.summary: Zooms or dollies in order to scale the viewport projection of observed objects.

params.name: mode
params.doc: false = perform a "dolly" magnification by moving the camera towards/away from the target so that the amount of the screen subtended by an object changes. true = perform a "zoom" magnification by adjusting the "lens" angle

params.name: fixedScreenPoint
params.doc: A point in the screen that should remain fixed.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: NextConstructionPlane
methods.summary: Sets the construction plane to the plane that was active before the last call to PreviousConstructionPlane.

returns.doc: true if successful.

methods.name: NextViewProjection
methods.summary: Sets the view projection and target to the settings that were active before the last call to PrevView.

returns.doc: true if the view stack was popped.

methods.name: PopConstructionPlane
methods.summary: Sets the construction plane to the plane that was active before the last call to PushConstructionPlane.

returns.doc: true if a construction plane was popped.

methods.name: PopViewProjection
methods.summary: Sets the view projection and target to the settings at the top of the view stack and removes those settings from the view stack.

returns.doc: true if there were settings that could be popped from the stack.

methods.name: PreviousConstructionPlane
methods.summary: Sets the construction plane to the plane that was active before the last call to NextConstructionPlane or SetConstructionPlane.

returns.doc: true if successful.

methods.name: PreviousViewProjection
methods.summary: Sets the view projection and target to the settings that were active before the last call to NextViewProjection.

returns.doc: true if the view stack was popped.

methods.name: PushConstructionPlane
methods.summary: Pushes the current construction plane on the viewport's construction plane stack and sets the construction plane to cplane.

methods.name: PushViewInfo
methods.signature: public bool PushViewInfo(ViewInfo viewinfo, bool includeTraceImage)

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Display.RhinoViewport.PushViewInfo(Rhino.DocObjects.ViewInfo,System.Boolean)"]

methods.name: PushViewProjection
methods.summary: Appends the current view projection and target to the viewport's view stack.

methods.name: Rotate
methods.signature: public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)
methods.summary: Rotates about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

params.name: rotationAxis
params.doc: direction of the axis of rotation.

params.name: rotationCenter
params.doc: point on the axis of rotation.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully rotated.
[Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Display.RhinoViewport.ScreenToClient(System.Drawing.Point)"]

methods.name: SetCameraDirection
methods.signature: public void SetCameraDirection(Vector3d cameraDirection, bool updateTargetLocation)
methods.summary: Set viewport camera direction. By default the target location is changed so that the vector from the camera location to the target is parallel to the camera direction.

params.name: updateTargetLocation
params.doc: if true, the target location is changed so that the vector from the camera location to the target is parallel to the camera direction. If false, the target location is not changed. See the remarks section of RhinoViewport.SetTarget for important details.

methods.name: SetCameraLocation
methods.signature: public void SetCameraLocation(Point3d cameraLocation, bool updateTargetLocation)
methods.summary: Set viewport camera location. By default the target location is changed so that the vector from the camera location to the target is parallel to the camera direction vector.

params.name: updateTargetLocation
params.doc: if true, the target location is changed so that the vector from the camera location to the target is parallel to the camera direction vector. If false, the target location is not changed. See the remarks section of RhinoViewport.SetTarget for important details.

methods.name: SetCameraLocations
methods.signature: public void SetCameraLocations(Point3d targetLocation, Point3d cameraLocation)
methods.summary: Set viewport camera location and target location. The camera direction vector is changed so that it is parallel to the vector from the camera location to the target location.

methods.name: SetCameraTarget
methods.signature: public void SetCameraTarget(Point3d targetLocation, bool updateCameraLocation)
methods.summary: Set viewport target point. By default the camera location is translated so that the camera direction vector is parallel to the vector from the camera location to the target location.
methods.remarks: In general, Rhino users expect to have the camera direction vector and the vector from the camera location to the target location to be parallel. If you use the RhinoViewport functions to set the camera location, camera direction, and target point, then the relationship between these three points and vectors is automatically maintained. If you directly manipulate the camera properties, then you should carefully set the target by calling SetTarget() with updateCameraLocation=false.

params.name: updateCameraLocation
params.doc: if true, the camera location is translated so that the camera direction vector is parallel to the vector from the camera location to the target location. If false, the camera location is not changed.

methods.name: SetClippingPlanes
methods.summary: Sets optimal clipping planes to view objects in a world coordinate 3d bounding box.

methods.name: SetConstructionPlane(ConstructionPlane)
methods.summary: Sets the construction plane to cplane.

methods.name: SetProjection
methods.signature: public bool SetProjection(DefinedViewportProjection projection, string viewName, bool updateConstructionPlane)
methods.summary: Set viewport to a defined projection.

params.name: viewName
params.doc: If not null or empty, the name is set.

params.name: updateConstructionPlane
params.doc: If true, the construction plane is set to the viewport plane.

returns.doc: true if successful.

params.doc: if true, the target location is changed so that the vector from the camera location to the target is parallel to the camera direction vector. If false, the target location is not changed.

methods.name: WorldToClient
methods.summary: Convert a point from world coordinates in the viewport to a 2d screen point in the local coordinates of the viewport (X/Y of point is relative to top left corner of viewport on screen)

returns.doc: The 2D point on the screen.

methods.name: ZoomBoundingBox
methods.summary: Zooms the viewport to the given bounding box.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: ZoomExtents
methods.summary: Dollies the camera location and so that the view frustum contains all of the selected document objects that can be seen in view. If the projection is perspective, the camera angle is not changed.

returns.doc: true if successful.

methods.name: ZoomExtentsSelected
methods.summary: Dollies the camera location and so that the view frustum contains all of the selected document objects that can be seen in view. If the projection is perspective, the camera angle is not changed.

returns.doc: true if successful.

properties.name: Bounds
properties.summary: Gets the size and location of the viewport, in pixels, relative to the parent view.

properties.name: CameraX
properties.summary: Gets the "unit to the right" vector.

properties.name: CameraZ
properties.summary: Gets the unit vector in CameraDirection.

properties.name: ChangeCounter
properties.summary: The value of change counter is incremented every time the view projection or construction plane changes. The user can the mouse and nestable view manipulation commands to change a view at any time. The value of change counter can be used to detect these changes in code that is sensitive to the view projection.

properties.name: FrustumAspect
properties.summary: Gets the width/height ratio of the frustum.

properties.name: IsPlanView
properties.summary: true if construction plane z axis is parallel to camera direction.

properties.name: ParentView
properties.summary: Gets the parent view, if there is one Every RhinoView has an associated RhinoViewport that does all the 3d display work. Those associated viewports return the RhinoView as their parent view. However, RhinoViewports are used in other image creating contexts that do not have a parent RhinoView. If you call ParentView, you MUST check for NULL return values.

properties.name: Size
properties.summary: Get or set the height and width of the viewport (in pixels)
